commit.message:
feat: intelligent reminder lead time based on task type

- Meeting/appointment: remind 30 min before (not at start time)
- Payment/bills: remind 3 days before
- Deadline/submission: remind 1 day before
- Travel/flight: remind 2 hours before
- Medical appointment: remind 1 hour before
- Other timed tasks: remind 15 min before
- No time specified: remind at 9am on due date

Also: reminder confirmation always shows actual remind time vs due time

commit.message:
fix: Docker entrypoint starts Claude in wrong working directory (#292)

The tmux session was created with `cd $HOME` (/home/zylos) instead of
`cd $ZYLOS_DIR` (/home/zylos/zylos). This caused Claude Code to not
find the project CLAUDE.md, breaking features that depend on it
(e.g., onboarding instructions).

`zylos init` correctly uses `cd $ZYLOS_DIR` (init.js line 720), but
the Docker entrypoint had its own tmux launch with the wrong path.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: time slots not showing in booking wizard

handleSelectVehicle was dispatching SET_VEHICLE (step→2) then NEXT_STEP
(step→3), skipping ServiceStep entirely. Service was never selected so
totalDuration=0, causing the availability API to reject the request
(duration must be positive) and return no slots.

Remove the redundant NEXT_STEP dispatch — SET_VEHICLE already advances
to step 2 via Math.max(state.step, 2).

Also add debug log to availability.ts for production visibility.

commit.message:
fix(pcb): correct BOM component values from reference board analysis

- Inductors L1/L2: 270nH → 27nH (C148128, Murata LQW18AN27NG00D)
  Previous value was 10× too high (~23Ω series impedance at 13.56MHz);
  reference board (Elechouse cardboard) confirmed uses 27nH.
- Crystal load caps C31/C32: 12pF → 10pF (C1634) to match crystal spec
- Damping resistors R1/R2: fix wrong LCSC part C22935 (was 1MΩ!) → C22939 (2.2Ω)
- Regenerate PCB, Gerbers, BOM.md with corrected values

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: generate config/prometheus.yml and nginx/nginx.conf as actual files

The generator referenced these files in docker-compose.yml volume mounts
but never wrote them to disk. Docker would create a directory at the missing
path and then fail to mount it as a file, causing:

  "not a directory: Are you trying to mount a directory onto a file?"

Fix: added generatePrometheusConfig() and generateNginxConfig() functions
and wired them into the task list:
- config/prometheus.yml is generated whenever Prometheus is enabled
- nginx/nginx.conf is generated for multi-node topology

Both are generated inline (no template engine) to avoid template function
dependency issues.

Add paper figure reproductions, README, and CLAUDE.md

- Add 5 new figures reproducing key figures from both papers:
  fig27: Steinberg Fig 3 (P1 Case 2 with m=3 wide, n=2 tall-narrow)
  fig28: Steinberg Fig 4 (tightness of 2*OPT bound, two sub-cases)
  fig29: Augustine Fig 1 (Omega(log n) lower bound, Lemma 4)

commit.message:
fix(bulls-and-cows): remove unnecessary build step from README

Removed go build/rm steps - we don't build Go apps, we just run them.

Changed:
- Removed: go build -o bulls-and-cows && rm bulls-and-cows
- Added: go mod tidy (generates go.sum, fixes dependencies)
- Simplified: start_core.sh uses 'go run *.go' automatically
- Moved manual start to separate section for clarity

The build step was causing errors because go.sum wasn't generated.
Standard workflow is: go mod tidy → go run *.go (via start_core.sh)

Add "早期解除する" button to the Wall's Zen Timer HTML page, allowing

break experience. Also fixes README wording to match the actual button
label.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Document symlink trick for whole-directory rw mounts

Directories with children in home_rw/ are mounted entry-by-entry,
causing new files (e.g. gh hosts.yml) to be lost on exit. Document
the workaround: symlink to an external directory so it mounts as
a single unit. Update setup example for .ssh and .config/gh.

commit.message: Fix Telegram formatting: switch to HTML parse mode Bot was sending LLM output with no parse_mode, so **bold** and *bullets* rendered as raw asterisks. - Add parse_mode=ParseMode.HTML to all reply_text calls in bot.py - Update system prompt to instruct the LLM to use bold for headers/final answer and plain hyphens for lists instead of markdown HTML chosen over MarkdownV2 because MarkdownV2 requires escaping dots, parentheses and hyphens — numbers in bill calculations would constantly break it. Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6

Add Phase 2 analysis: text temperature, cross-industry competition, anomaly detection, cascade summary

- A-1: Anomaly detection (2σ outliers per metric per emp_group)
- S-1: Cascade analysis (industry × emp_group comprehensive table)
- C-4: Added industry-level vacancy rate breakdown
- Fixed .into() → .to_string() compilation issues
- All metrics segmented by employment type (正社員/パート/その他)

commit.message:
[feat] Finalize status msg into completed group instead of delete+resend

Status message flow:
1. Status msg shows live progress (edit in place)
2. When group completes, status msg is edited into the finished group
   (💬 thought + expandable tools) — stays in place, no deletion
3. New status msg sent below for next work
4. On completion, final status deleted, clean answer below

No more delete+resend flicker. Each completed group is just the
previous status message edited into its final form.

commit.message:
🛡️ Sentinel: [HIGH] Fix IDOR in blog post deletion (#622)

* 🛡️ Sentinel: Fix IDOR vulnerability in blog post deletion

- Added explicit `.eq('merchant_id', merchant.id)` check to `delete()` query.
- Implemented error handling for the Supabase response to prevent silent failures.
- Wrapped the operation in a `try...catch` block with a safe user-facing error message.

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: extend IDOR protection to read/update queries and add tests

Address CodeRabbit review findings:
- Add merchant_id scoping to fetchPost read query (defense-in-depth)
- Add merchant_id scoping and null guard to handleSave update mutation
- Add blank line before heading in sentinel.md (markdownlint MD022)
- Add colocated test file for delete handler tenant isolation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add 26 example figures covering simple cases, edge cases, and stress tests

Figures organized in 5 categories:
- A (fig01-05): Simple cases — single rect, side-by-side, paper example,
  P1 trigger, P_{-1} trigger
- B (fig06-10): Core algorithms — Steinberg 15 rects, chain/diamond/tree/fan DAGs
- C (fig11-18): Edge cases — all width=1, tiny rects, one large + many tiny,
  width=0.5 boundary, mixed sizes, identical, deep chain 50, chain-of-groups
- D (fig19-20): Analysis — approximation ratio plot, DAG structure comparison
- E (fig21-26): Multi-panel galleries, Steinberg vs naive, bound scatter plot

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
